signal-town-cold-signal-open: Outside, the mailbox flag snaps up once. Inside is a photo of Bosch walking uphill before sunrise.
Bosch was asleep when it was taken. MiFella saw the same figure near the meteor.
Follow the north road uphill and inspect the cold signal. Then trace the copy back toward the MONS LINK arcade.
onett-brother-fallsin: MiFella catches up beside the meteor.
"The other Bosch stopped here. When I got close, that blue signal peeled away from the wreckage."
"I will watch the road. Check the terminal, then follow whatever it points to."

signal-town-card-clique: At the MONS LINK arcade, kids are trading cards with Bosch's face on them. The cards were here before he woke up.
CARD CLIQUE: "The clean Bosch already signed. You are the late copy."
Bosch holds up the damaged Dox Sheet. The signatures match, but nobody in the arcade admits writing either one.
signal-town-card-clique-reveal: The card printer stops. Inside its tray is a route pass prepared for Bosch before the fight began.
The pass is stamped for the civic gate beside THE PRECINCT. The stamp has a blank space where an author should be.
Bosch keeps both records. Follow the main street east, then uphill to the station gate.
relay-gate-returnless-king: The station gate scans the clean card and opens. It scans Bosch and locks again.
RETURNLESS KING: "One Bosch is already approved. Sign here and we can discard the duplicate."
Bosch refuses. The gate will not identify who approved the card, so he forces the route open.
north-route-gate-warning: The road is visible beyond the station, but a line of route aides blocks it.
"The gate already approved Bosch. If that is you, prove it at the desk."
first-threshold-malady: At the crossing beside the hotel, the other Bosch is waiting. Its clothes are spotless. Its smile begins a moment too early.
PUBLIC BOSCH: "Everyone recognized me first. That makes you the copy."
Bosch asks what happened in his bedroom. The Public Bosch repeats the question but cannot answer it.
The clean face folds flat into a laminated record and reaches for the damaged Dox Sheet. Bosch fights to keep the one record it cannot complete.
first-threshold-malady-reveal: The Public Bosch comes apart. Beneath the clean face is a stack of cards, gate stamps, and copied details from people Bosch met today.
Every piece carries the same blank authorization mark. Someone coordinated the copy, but nothing here names them.
The damaged Dox Sheet records a rough first track. Munch is waiting a few steps beyond the crossing.
recruit-munch: Munch steps out from behind the hotel sign with a pocket full of rejected receipts.
MUNCH: "That copy knew what strangers know about you. It missed what your friends know. I can help find the seams."
Munch joins the crew. He can SPY on enemies to reveal what they resist and what hurts them.
leave-signal-town: At the town exit, a clerk offers Bosch a clean replacement Dox Sheet. Signing it would make the Public Bosch the official version.
Bosch hands back the pen and keeps the damaged sheet.
CLERK: "Then your record remains unresolved. The same blank authorization appears in Postwick. Start there."

objectiveTextById.act1-card-clique: Go west to the MONS LINK arcade and confront the kids carrying Bosch's picture.
objectiveTextById.act1-returnless-king: Take the arcade route pass east, then uphill to the civic gate beside THE PRECINCT.
objectiveTextById.act1-malady: Follow the opened road to the crossing beside the hotel and confront the Public Bosch.
objectiveTextById.act1-munch: Meet Munch just beyond the hotel crossing before leaving Morningside.
objectiveTextById.act1-leave-signal-town: Continue past the hotel crossing to Morningside's exit desk.
objectiveTextById.act2-reach-postwick: Follow the open route from Morningside until you reach Postwick.

pages: "That flash came from the bedroom window. Then MiFella started pounding on the door."
"Change clothes, meet him outside, and stay together on the hill."
"I saw somebody with your face cross the yard before sunrise. You were still in bed."
"MiFella followed him uphill. Get dressed and find MiFella outside."
"The other Bosch never looked at the family pictures. He walked straight to the road like he already knew the route."
"He went north toward the meteor. That is the first place I would look."
The house phone rings once, then stops.
MiFella left a message: "I saw the other Bosch head uphill toward the meteor. Meet me outside."
"The crossing by the hotel started humming before sunrise. Then someone with your face took up position there."
"The station gate controls that road. Start with the gate."
"A clean copy of my question came back before I asked it. Same words, none of the worry."
"Your clean card has that feeling. Find out who stamped it."
"The route aides only look at cards. Faces make them nervous."
"The civic gate beside THE PRECINCT tells them which card counts."
"I saw the arcade printer spit out a pass for you before the doors opened."
"Its destination stamp points to the station gate."
"Your clean version smiles like it practiced from a picture."
"Ask it about something no picture could know."
"A form can be wrong and still block the road. That is what scares me."
"Keep the damaged sheet. At least its mistakes happened to you."
"People keep repeating stories about the other Bosch. Every version is more confident than the last."
"Confidence is not memory. Ask for a detail."
"The arcade kids say your card is official. They also say the printer chose them personally."
"Printers do not choose people. Somebody loaded the tray."
"My feed says you won a fight you have not fought yet. It included a picture."
"The picture shows the hotel crossing. That is north of the station gate."
"Something with your face is waiting beyond the gate."
"It arrived with a route pass. The gate never asked where the pass came from."
"Remilia is holding the stamp today. She did not write the standing order."
"The order has no signature, only the same blank mark as your card."
"Before sunrise, the arcade received a box of cards with your face on them."
"MONS LINK is west along this road, beside SLICE. Start there."
"A clean version of me replaced my shift before lunch. It knew my schedule, not my bad knee."
"Copies learn records first. People learn each other slower."
"Little Swag World used to be a place, not a claim. Now every bottle says it was first."
"Keep the bottle with the crooked label. I filled that one myself."
"Those cards read you back. Watch what they get wrong."
"The arcade printer is inside MONS LINK, just west of here."
"The figure beyond the gate has your face. It keeps asking strangers what you are like."
"The crossing is beside the hotel, once the station opens the road."
"The gate is not judging your face. It is matching your card to a standing order."
"Your arcade pass carries the mark it wants."
MiFella pounds on the door.
"Bosch, get up. I saw you outside ten minutes ago, but you were still asleep."
"The other you went uphill toward the meteor. I followed until the signal started moving."
"Get dressed and meet me outside. We need to see what came down."
A terminal wakes inside the wreckage.
LOG: SECOND BOSCH ENTERED MORNINGSIDE BEFORE ORIGINAL LEFT HOME.
The copy has your face, but it does not know your history. People who know you can still tell.
The signal points west toward the MONS LINK arcade. Find the kids carrying your picture.
"MiFella called. The signal left the meteor and the arcade kids have your picture."
"MONS LINK is west along the town road, beside SLICE."
"The clean Bosch never looked at these family pictures. You did, on the way back in."
"Whatever that thing was, knowing your face was not the same as knowing you."
"The thing at the meteor could not answer MiFella's question. That matters."
"Take what you found to the arcade west of town."
"The arcade pass names the civic gate. That is a real lead, even if the signature is blank."
"Go east, then uphill to THE PRECINCT."
"The other Bosch went from the meteor toward the arcade."
"MONS LINK is west on the main road, beside SLICE."
The house phone rings.
Munch left a message: "The road is open. The other Bosch is at the crossing beside the hotel."
The house phone rings.
MiFella left a message: "The arcade pass points to the civic gate beside THE PRECINCT."
The house phone rings.
MiFella left a message: "The signal moved west to MONS LINK. The arcade kids have your picture."
"The crossing stopped humming when that clean face fell apart."
"I saw the blank mark inside it. Somebody wanted every piece to look ownerless."
"The gate opened. The other Bosch is waiting at the crossing beside the hotel."
"Ask it something a photograph cannot answer."
"The clean Bosch was made from cards, stamps, and things we told strangers."
"You kept the damaged record. Good. Damage has a history."
"The route aides moved. The crossing by the hotel is open now."
"Someone with your face is standing there."
"The arcade printer stopped, but its route pass is real."
"Take it east, then uphill to the gate beside THE PRECINCT."
"The cards are blank now. The last one printed a pass for the station gate."
"Go east, then uphill. Make the desk explain who approved it."
"The other Bosch collapsed into records from all over town."
"It knew what we said about you. It did not know what happened to you."
"The road is clear. Your copy is at the crossing beside the hotel."
"Do not let the smile fool you. It has been practicing."
"You forced the gate to admit its order had no author."
"Follow the open road to the hotel crossing. The approved Bosch is waiting."
